title: Handling rrc connection release
abstract: According to certain embodiments, a method for use in a wireless device comprises receiving a release message from a network node operating in a network. The release message comprises a first release token and an instruction to release a radio resource control (RRC) connection. The method comprises determining whether the first release token passes verification and determining an action to perform based on whether the first release token passes verification. In response to a determination that the first release token passes verification, the action comprises releasing the RRC connection.
claims: Claims 1. A method for use in a wireless device, the method comprising: receiving (1 10, 602) a release message from a network node operating in a network, wherein the release message comprises a first release token and an instruction to release a radio resource control (RRC) connection; determining (1 12, 604) whether the first release token passes verification; and determining (1 14, 606) an action to perform based on whether the first release token passes verification; wherein, in response to a determination that the first release token passes verification, the action comprises releasing the RRC connection. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the RRC connection comprises a connection for which access stratum (AS) security has not been established. 3. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the RRC connection comprises a connection for which data radio bearers (DRBs) have not been established. 4. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the RRC connection supports Control Plane Cellular Internet-of-Things (CP-CloT) or Data over Non Access Stratum (DoNAS) functionality. 5. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the release message is received via a signaling radio bearer (SRB) that lacks access stratum security. 6. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the first release token is received via access stratum (AS) signaling. 7. The method of any of claims 1 -5, wherein the first release token is received via non access stratum (NAS) signaling. 8. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein in response to a determination that the first release token fails verification, the action comprises starting a procedure to re-establish the RRC connection. 9. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein in response to a determination that the first release token fails verification, the action comprises ignoring the instruction to release the RRC connection. 10. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein determining whether the first release token passes verification comprises: determining that the first release token passes verification if the first release token received in the release message matches a second release token obtained by the wireless device. 1 1. The method of any of the previous claims, further comprising computing the second release token based at least in part on a key shared between the wireless device and the network. 12. The method of any of the previous claims, further comprising computing the second release token based at least in part on an algorithm agreed between the wireless device and the network. 13. The method of any of the previous claims, further comprising, prior to receiving the release message, obtaining information indicating that the wireless device is to use token-based verification of the release message. 14. The method of any of the previous claims, further comprising, prior to receiving the release message, obtaining an indication indicating that a key, an algorithm, or both are in place for computing the second release token. 15. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the indication indicating that the key, the algorithm, or both are in place for computing the second release token is obtained implicitly. 16. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the indication indicating that the key, the algorithm, or both are in place for computing the second release token is obtained explicitly. 17. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the indication indicating that the key, the algorithm, or both are in place for computing the second release token is received from a radio access network node. 18. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the indication indicating that the key, the algorithm, or both are in place for computing the second release token is received from a core network node. 19. A wireless device (210, 300) comprising processing circuitry (212, 315) and logic that, when executed by the processing circuitry, causes the wireless device to: receive a release message from a network node operating in a network, wherein the release message comprises a first release token and an instruction to release a radio resource control (RRC) connection; determine whether the first release token passes verification; and determine an action to perform based on whether the first release token passes verification; wherein, in response to a determination that the first release token passes verification, the action comprises releasing the RRC connection. 20. The wireless device of claim 19, wherein the RRC connection comprises a connection for which access stratum (AS) security has not been established. 21. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein the RRC connection comprises a connection for which data radio bearers (DRBs) have not been established. 22. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein the RRC connection supports Control Plane Cellular Internet-of-Things (CP-CloT) or Data over Non Access Stratum (DoNAS) functionality. 23. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein the release message is received via a signaling radio bearer (SRB) that lacks access stratum security. 24. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein the first release token is received via access stratum (AS) signaling. 25. The wireless device of any of claims 19-23, wherein the first release token is received via non access stratum (NAS) signaling. 26. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein in response to a determination that the first release token fails verification, the action comprises starting a procedure to re-establish the RRC connection. 27. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein in response to a determination that the first release token fails verification, the action comprises ignoring the instruction to release the RRC connection. 28. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein to determine whether the first release token passes verification, the wireless device is configured to: determine that the first release token passes verification if the first release token received in the release message matches a second release token obtained by the wireless device. 29. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein the wireless device is configured to compute the second release token based at least in part on a key shared between the wireless device and the network. 30. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein the wireless device is configured to compute the second release token based at least in part on an algorithm agreed between the wireless device and the network. 31. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein prior to receiving the release message, the wireless device is configured to receive an indication from a radio access network node associated with the network, the indication indicating that a key, an algorithm, or both are in place for computing the second release token. 32. The wireless device of any of the previous claims, wherein prior to receiving the release message, the wireless device is further configured to receive an indication from a core network node associated with the network, the indication indicating that a key, an algorithm, or both are in place for computing the second release token. 33. A computer program, the computer program comprising logic which when executed on a computer causes the computer to perform: receiving (1 10, 602) a release message from a network node operating in a network, wherein the release message comprises a first release token and an instruction to release a radio resource control (RRC) connection; determining (1 12, 604) whether the first release token passes verification; and determining (1 14, 606) an action to perform based on whether the first release token passes verification; wherein, in response to a determination that the first release token passes verification, the action comprises releasing the RRC connection. 34. A method for use in a network node operating in a network, the method comprising: obtaining (108, 702) a first release token; and sending (1 10, 704) a release message to a wireless device, wherein the release message comprises the first release token and an instruction to release a radio resource control (RRC) connection. 35. The method of claim 34, wherein the RRC connection comprises a connection for which access stratum (AS) security has not been established. 36. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the RRC connection comprises a connection for which data radio bearers (DRBs) have not been established. 37. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the RRC connection supports Control Plane Cellular Internet-of-Things (CP-CloT) or Data over Non Access Stratum (DoNAS) functionality. 38. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the release message is sent via a signaling radio bearer (SRB) that lacks access stratum security. 39. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the network node comprises a base station and the first release token is sent via access stratum (AS) signaling. 40. The method of any of claims 34-38, wherein the network node comprises a core network node and the first release token is sent via non access stratum (NAS) signaling. 41. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein obtaining the first release token comprises obtaining the first release token from another network node. 42. The method of claim 41 , wherein the method is implemented in a base station and the first release token is obtained from a core network node. 43. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein obtaining the first release token comprise computing the first release token. 44. The method of claim 43, wherein the first release token is computed based at least in part on information received from another network node. 45. The method any of claims 43 or 44, wherein the first release token is computed based at least in part based at least in part on a key shared between the wireless device and the network. 46. The method of any of claims 43-45, wherein the first release token is computed based at least in part on an algorithm agreed between the wireless device and the network. 47. The method of any of claims 34-39 or 41 -46, wherein the method is performed by a radio access network (RAN) node. 48. The method of any of claims 34-38, 40-41 , or 43-46, wherein the method is performed by a core network node. 49. The method of any of the previous claims, wherein the release message is sent in response to detecting a radio link failure (RLF) associated with the RRC connection. 50. The method of any of the previous claims, further comprising: determining whether to include the first release token in the release message based on whether the release message is being sent via a signaling radio bearer (SRB) that lacks access stratum security. 51. The method of any of the previous claims, further comprising, prior to sending the release message, sending information indicating that the wireless device is to use token-based verification of the release message. 52. The method of any of the previous claims, further comprising, prior to receiving the release message, sending an indication indicating that a key, an algorithm, or both are in place for computing the second release token. 53. A network node (200a, 200b) comprising processing circuitry (202) and logic that, when executed by the processing circuitry, causes the network node to: obtain a first release token; and send a release message to a wireless device, wherein the release message comprises the first release token and an instruction to release a radio resource control (RRC) connection. 54. The network node of claim 53, wherein the RRC connection comprises a connection for which access stratum (AS) security has not been established. 55. The network node of any of the previous claims, wherein the RRC connection comprises a connection for which data radio bearers (DRBs) have not been established. 56. The network node of any of the previous claims, wherein the RRC connection supports Control Plane Cellular Internet-of-Things (CP-CloT) or Data over Non Access Stratum (DoNAS) functionality. 57. The network node of any of the previous claims, wherein the release message is sent via a signaling radio bearer (SRB) that lacks access stratum security. 58. The network node of any of the previous claims, wherein the network node comprises a base station and the first release token is sent via access stratum (AS) signaling. 59. The network node of any of claims 53-57, wherein the network node comprises a core network node and the first release token is sent via non access stratum (NAS) signaling. 60. The network node of any of the previous claims, wherein obtaining the first release token comprises obtaining the first release token from another network node. 61. The network node of claim 60, wherein the method is implemented in a base station and the first release token is obtained from a core network node. 62. The network node of any of the previous claims, wherein obtaining the first release token comprise computing the first release token. 63. The network node of claim 62, wherein the first release token is computed based at least in part on information received from another network node. 64. The method any of claims 62 or 63, wherein the first release token is computed based at least in part based at least in part on a key shared between the wireless device and the network. 65. The network node of any of claims 62-64, wherein the first release token is computed based at least in part on an algorithm agreed between the wireless device and the network. 66. The network node of any of claims 53-58 or 60-66, wherein the network node comprises a radio access network (RAN) node. 67. The network node of any of claims 53-57, 59-60, or 62-66, wherein network node comprises a core network node. 68. The network node of any of the previous claims, wherein the release message is sent in response to detecting a radio link failure (RLF) associated with the RRC connection. 69. The network node of any of the previous claims, further configured to determine whether to include the first release token in the release message based on whether the release message is being sent via a signaling radio bearer (SRB) that lacks access stratum security. 70. A computer program, the computer program comprising logic which when executed on a computer causes the computer to perform: obtaining (108, 702) a first release token; and sending (1 10, 704) a release message to a wireless device, wherein the release message comprises the first release token and an instruction to release a radio resource control (RRC) connection.
